CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-23-2023 Set For Trial On Mar-25-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEFENDANT MICHAEL BURKS DEMURER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2-DEFENDANT MICHAEL BURKS DEMURER TO COMPLAINT.

Off calendar as a duplicate entry.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

DEFENDANT MICHAEL BURKS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

-DEFENDANT MICHAEL BURKS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Defendant's demurrer is overruled.

The parties agree that the demurrer turns on three issues.

First, the complaint adequately pleads that plaintiff contributed to the trust. (Id. at 2:9, 3:9-15.)

Second, Probate Code 15401(b)(1) provides that if, as here, "a trust is created by more than one settlor, each settlor may revoke the trust as to the portion of the trust contributed by that settlor." (Id.; see also Estate of Powell (2000) 83 Cal.App.4th 1434, 1440 [1994 legislative amendment to Probate Code 15401 "reversed prior judicial decisions holding that a trust could not be revoked by less than all joint settlors"].) Plaintiff adequately pleads she revoked the portion of the trust she contributed. (Cmplt. 2:6-10, 4:14-17.)

Third, because she pled contribution to the trust and revocation of her portion of it, plaintiff now co-owns the trust property in her individual capacity and adequately pleads quiet title and partition claims, which can be adjudicated sequentially. (Cmplt. 5:11-6:22.)
Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
